change: you change how messages are classified (past/present/future in checkMessage)

impact.why: The classifier itself returns errFutureMessage/errOldMessage/errExtraSealMessage/errInvalidMessage; the routing decisions downstream depend on its exact return-code semantics.
Calls checkMessage and branches on errFutureMessage -> addToBacklog and errExtraSealMessage -> addToExtraSeal; the seed loop's classification routing lives here.
Re-runs checkMessage on backlogged messages; treats errFutureMessage as 'still future' (push back) and errExtraSealMessage as deliverable. Reclassification changes drain behavior.
Decode+verify path that feeds handleDecodedMessage; the MessageEvent branch of handleEvents enters here.
Invoked only after checkMessage passes (present message); dispatches to per-type handlers. Classification gates whether this runs.
Future messages are stored here; priority via toNegatePriority. If future-classification rules change, what gets stored changes.
Backlog ordering depends on RoundChange-vs-other distinction mirroring checkMessage's RoundChange special-casing; must stay consistent with classification.
Every state change calls processBacklog(); state-dependent checkMessage branches (StateAcceptRequest/Preprepared/Prepared/Committed) mean reclassification interacts with state transitions here.
Starts the handleEvents goroutine and owns the backlog/event lifecycle the seed loop runs in.

grep_would_miss: grep 'handleEvents' finds only the loop body and its launcher; it misses checkMessage, the actual classification logic, which is in backlog.go not handler.go.
The errExtraSealMessage path (a 4th classification beyond past/present/future) is easy to miss — it routes to addToExtraSeal, not the backlog.
processBacklog re-invokes checkMessage; grep on 'checkMessage' catches it but grep on 'handleEvents' does not, so the backlog-drain coupling is invisible from the seed name.
setState -> processBacklog coupling: classification semantics are state-sensitive, but setState never mentions checkMessage or handleEvents.
toNegatePriority's RoundChange special-case must stay in sync with checkMessage's RoundChange branch — a semantic dependency with no shared identifier to grep.
notes: Seed handleEvents (handler.go:102-181) excluded as the loop definition, but its routing into handleDecodedMessage is the classification entry point and is included. Real classifier is checkMessage. Backlog trio (addToBacklog/processBacklog/setState) forms the past/future handling. _test.go skipped.
